key_uncertainties: European deployment economics vs. Caribbean baseline
2025-Q2.mgmt_statement_summary: Included in the seven-ship introduction plan over the next several years, with Legend of the Seas slated for 2026 between Xcel (2025) and Icon 4 / first Celebrity River (2027).

status_delta.detail: first quarter this asset appears in the lifecycle record

key_uncertainties: share of the 6% 2026 capacity growth attributable to Legend
2025-Q3.mgmt_statement_summary: 2026 capacity expected up ~6%, driven by Legend's European summer debut plus full-year Star and Xcel.

key_uncertainties: Europe at 15% of capacity growing 5% — yield discipline risk
2025-Q4.mgmt_statement_summary: Delivery in Q2 2026 with committed financing in place; strong booking trends; first Icon-class ship to debut in Europe. Total 2026 capacity now framed at +6.7% year-over-year.

key_uncertainties: transition from European summer to Caribbean winter (Nov redeploy)
Caribbean pricing absorption of an incremental Icon-class ship
2026-Q1.mgmt_statement_summary: Third Icon-class ship; Europe summer 2026 debut followed by Caribbean redeployment in November; cited as easier Q4 comp and a structural tailwind alongside a fuller year of Star.

primary_framings_observed: Booking strength and yield premium of Icon class
Europe debut economics and Caribbean redeployment as Q4 tailwind

currently_open_questions_as_of_2026Q1: Does Legend match Star and Xcel on booked APD and load factor in its inaugural European season?
What is the yield delta between Legend's European summer deployment and the November Caribbean redeployment?
How does Legend reshape the 2026 Q4 like-for-like comparison versus 2025 Q4?
Has committed financing materially altered the per-berth capital cost relative to Star/Xcel?
